summary: DHH argues that the return-to-office mandates from large tech companies have restored remote work as a competitive hiring advantage for smaller companies and startups. He notes that after the pandemic, many workers now consider remote work a non-negotiable job requirement. While the current soft job market gives big companies leverage, he predicts this will shift as the market tightens. He frames remote work as an enabling technology comparable to open-source software that lowers barriers to new company formation.
key_points: Large tech companies forcing return-to-office has reopened a competitive hiring gap that benefits remote-first smaller companies
Many workers now treat remote work as a defining, non-negotiable characteristic of a job, especially those outside major tech hubs
The current soft tech job market temporarily masks the hiring disadvantage of requiring office attendance
Remote work functions as an enabling technology that lowers the cost of starting new companies, similar to open-source software
The bifurcation between office-first big companies and remote-first small companies is a net positive for entrepreneurship
money_quotes: If you're trying to compete for talent with the likes of Google or Apple, you need all the help you can get, and these companies forcing everyone back into an office might just be your biggest lift.
For quite a few people, working remotely or not has become the defining characteristic of a job.
The world is full of wonderful, talented people who won't do this.
Eventually the tech job market is going to tighten again.
Remote work is an enabling technology, like free open-source software, which lowers the cost for new-company formation.
Granting more entrepreneurs more opportunities to take on the stodgy establishment without the need for other people's money.
key_insight: Return-to-office mandates by big tech companies are inadvertently creating a structural hiring advantage for remote-first startups, functioning as an enabling technology that democratizes access to talent.
